name: Coeur de Gascogne – Auch loop
name_original: Coeur de Gascogne – boucle de Auch
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Auch traverse the scenic Gascon countryside, characterized by rolling hills, vineyards, and orchards. The region offers varied terrain suitable for different fitness levels, with moderate elevation gains and some challenging climbs. Distant views of the Pyrenees mountains are visible on clear days, adding to the landscape. The Gers River flows through Auch, providing a natural greenway for cycling within the city. Best road cycling routes around Auch The most popular road cycling route is Foie Gras Circuit, a 41.4 miles (66.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 22 minutes to complete. This moderate route features the picturesque Gers countryside. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Auch and the Moulin de Durban Loop, a moderate 32.7 miles (52.6 km) path. It offers views of the Pyrenees and passes through the Gascon countryside. Local road cyclists also love the Bastides and Castelnaux Trail: Gimont Loop, a 53.9 miles (86.8 km) trail leading through historic bastides and castelnaux, often completed in about 4 hours 11 minutes. Road cycling around Auch is defined by rolling hills, vineyards, and historic bastides. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to more challenging routes. The routes in Auch are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 300 reviews. More than 6,700 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Auch's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Auch?
A: The road cycling routes around Auch primarily feature the scenic Gascon countryside, characterized by rolling hills, vineyards, and orchards. While the terrain is generally undulating, offering moderate elevation gains, some routes present more challenging climbs. On clear days, you can also enjoy distant views of the Pyrenees mountains.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or those seeking an easier ride?
A: Yes, Auch offers a variety of routes for different skill levels. For an easier ride, consider the shorter &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1276400667/foie-gras-circuit" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Foie Gras Circuit&lt;/a&gt;, which is approximately 18.5 miles (29.8 km) long with moderate elevation. Another easy option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24670275" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;13th century bridge – Auch loop from Auch&lt;/a&gt;, covering about 24.5 miles (39.5 km) with minimal elevation gain.

Q: What are some challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders in the Auch area?
A: For experienced cyclists looking for a challenge, Auch offers several demanding routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1276378509/la-tenace-loop-in-the-gers" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Tenace — loop in the Gers&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult route spanning over 77 miles (125 km) with significant elevation. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1275604017/coeur-de-gascogne-auch-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coeur de Gascogne – Auch loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is over 79 miles (128 km) long and features substantial climbs.

Q: What historical landmarks or attractions can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: The region around Auch is rich in history. Many routes pass through historic bastides and castelnaux. You can encounter notable sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1473257" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lavardens Castle&lt;/a&gt;, a magnificent 17th-century structure, or the charming &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1437059" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bastide and Cologne hall&lt;/a&gt;. The city of Auch itself boasts the monumental staircase, the Tower of Armagnac, and the Sainte-Marie Cathedral, a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

Q: Are there any scenic viewpoints or natural features to look out for?
A: Absolutely. The routes offer picturesque views of the Gascon countryside, including vineyards and orchards. On clear days, you'll be treated to distant views of the Pyrenees mountains. Within Auch, the Gers River provides a shaded greenway, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/491846" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Château de Montégut&lt;/a&gt; offers a lovely panorama.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling around Auch?
A: The region's 'green fields and fresh air' make it appealing for cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summer can be warm, but early mornings or late afternoons are often ideal. Always check local weather conditions before heading out.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around Auch are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1268923011/auch-and-the-moulin-de-durban-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Auch and the Moulin de Durban Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1276194092/bastides-and-castelnaux-trail-gimont-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bastides and Castelnaux Trail: Gimont Loop&lt;/a&gt;, both offering a complete circuit.

Q: What do other cyclists say about road cycling in Auch?
A: The road cycling routes in Auch are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 300 reviews. More than 6,700 road cyclists have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the scenic Gascon countryside, rolling hills, and the blend of natural beauty with historic sites.

Q: Can I find places to eat or drink along the cycling routes?
A: While specific cafes or pubs directly on every route aren't detailed, the region is known for its culinary heritage. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1276341794/foie-gras-circuit" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Foie Gras Circuit&lt;/a&gt; hint at gastronomic delights. You'll find opportunities to explore local markets, such as the Marché au gras in Gimont, or visit towns and villages along your ride for refreshments and local produce.

Q: Are there options for family-friendly cycling in Auch?
A: Within Auch itself, the city prioritizes 'soft mobility' and offers dedicated cycle routes along the banks of the Gers River. The Promenade Claude Desbons is a greenway suitable for both pedestrians and cyclists, providing a pleasant and safe way to experience the city's natural setting, which can be a good option for families.

Q: How can I get to the road cycling routes using public transport?
A: Auch is the historic capital of Gascony and serves as a central point. While specific public transport links directly to the start of every rural cycling route might be limited, you can often reach Auch by regional transport. For detailed information on local public transport options to access the wider region, it's advisable to consult the local tourism office or transport providers directly.

Q: Where can I find parking for my road bike near the routes?
A: Auch and the surrounding towns generally offer parking facilities. For routes starting directly in Auch, you can look for public parking within the city. When venturing into smaller villages for specific route starts, parking is typically available, though it might be more limited. Always check local signage for parking regulations.

Q: What is the typical duration for road cycling routes around Auch?
A: The duration varies significantly depending on the route's length and difficulty. For instance, a moderate route like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1268923011/auch-and-the-moulin-de-durban-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Auch and the Moulin de Durban Loop&lt;/a&gt; (32.7 miles / 52.6 km) typically takes around 3 hours. More challenging and longer routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1275604017/coeur-de-gascogne-auch-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coeur de Gascogne – Auch loop&lt;/a&gt; (79.6 miles / 128 km), can take over 6 hours to complete.
tips: Old 13th century humpback bridge spanning the Gers, approved by King Philippe the Bold in 1285.
L'Isle-de-Noé is located at the junction of the Petite Baïse and Grande Baïse rivers. In particular, you can discover a 13th century church, as well as a bridge, and an 18th century castle.

The bastide town of Bassoues (pronounced Bassous) is a fortified village in the Gers with a keep 40 meters high which attests to the existence of the fortress of the bishops of Auch. Built on top of a hill, the city can be seen from afar.

Nice view of the village.
In the old town you will find a few cafes and restaurants.

The Castle, built in the 17th century. has been listed as a Historic Monument since 1961.

Prefecture of the Gers department.

Renowned for its gastronomy, its cathedral, and its staircase from the top of which the statue of d'Artagnan dominates the course of the Gers.
